demographics.audit_notes: Extreme population growth (+167% from 2010) is confirmed by 2020 Census data, reflecting hyper-development in the region (e.g., Tesla Gigafactory).
Continued rapid population growth is plausible given regional development.
Population data aligns with latest ACS estimates, confirming continued hyper-growth.
Projections based on linear extrapolation of 2020-2022 ACS trend. High growth is plausible but uncertain.
Projections based on linear extrapolation of 2020-2022 ACS trend. High growth is plausible but uncertain.
Projections based on linear extrapolation of 2020-2022 ACS trend. High growth is plausible but uncertain.

property.audit_notes: Corrected homeownership_rate from 72% to 74.6% to match US Census 1990 data (1058 owner-occupied / 1419 occupied units).
Corrected homeownership_rate from 70.5% to 73.5% based on interpolation of corrected 1990 and 2000 census values.
Corrected homeownership_rate from 68% to 72.4% to match US Census 2000 data (1789 owner-occupied / 2471 occupied units).
Original homeownership_rate was plausible despite corrected 2000 value; retained as medium confidence.
Housing unit growth is extremely high, reflecting development boom.
Extreme YoY home value change reflects post-pandemic housing market spike. New construction permit estimate is extremely high but reflects observed hyper-growth.
Housing unit count confirmed by ACS 2018-2022. YoY home value change remains extremely high.
Slight dip in median home value aligns with broader Austin market correction in 2023.
Projected values show market stabilization and continued housing growth.
Projected values show market stabilization and continued housing growth.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Unemployment rate reflects impact of the Great Recession.
Unemployment rate spike reflects the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.
Rapid income and education gains reflect the changing demographics of the area.
Projections based on 2022 ACS data and regional economic trends.
